title: Executive Order 9427—Establishment of the Retraining and Reemployment Administration

flags.justification: The Administrator operates under Director of War Mobilization supervision with a nine-agency Policy Board assisting — a distributed, checked design.

A clear supervisory chain exists, and Sec. 2(a) explicitly directs the Administration to 'advise with the appropriate committees of the Congress' — a genuine, textually built-in accountability channel.
Related sub-functions (retraining, reemployment, veterans' care) coherently serve one purpose: postwar transition planning.
This body coordinates and directs other government agencies' activities rather than creating a binding compliance regime against private third parties.
No direct third-party compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.

The Administration's function is inherently temporary — managing the transition from wartime to peacetime employment — yet no explicit termination trigger or review point is specified, unlike other war-powers-tied orders in this corpus with explicit statutory-expiration end dates.
Sec. 5's supersession of conflicting prior orders is standard housekeeping; no external oversight is removed.
No direct third-party compliance regime exists; the Policy Board and congressional-advisory function provide the relevant oversight instead.
Establishes a significant, forward-looking coordinating framework for postwar veteran and worker transition — a real institutional precedent for later reemployment and veterans'-transition programs.

Sec. 4 explicitly directs that the Administration's functions 'shall be performed through existing Government agencies and officials so far as feasible' — designed to work through, not displace, existing agencies.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
